level_signals: Comp range $305K-$385K base, well above typical Senior PM, consistent with Staff/Principal PM at Anthropic
Expected to own strategy and roadmap independently and communicate to senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience, with deep exposure to compute infrastructure, distributed systems, or scheduling/orchestration platforms
must_haves.qualifier: with deep exposure to compute infrastructure, distributed systems, or scheduling/orchestration platforms

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience taking technical infrastructure products from infancy to scale—you've built something from the ground up and grown it to serve demanding internal or external customers

Track record of building platform products that balance the needs of multiple users and stakeholders—you're comfortable making prioritization trade-offs between utilization, latency, cost, and fairness, and communicating them clearly
Ability to internalize complex technical systems (job schedulers, cluster managers, resource orchestrators) and translate that understanding into a comprehensive product vision
Fluent across functions—you're equally credible discussing scheduling algorithms with engineers, capacity economics with finance, and infrastructure strategy with leadership
Strong instinct for connecting technical decisions to business outcomes: every percentage point of cluster utilization has measurable impact
Scrappy and resourceful—you do what it takes to get things done in a fast-moving environment
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Built or scaled job scheduling, resource orchestration, or workload management systems for large-scale compute clusters (e.g., Kubernetes, Slurm, Borg, YARN, or custom schedulers).
Deep familiarity with GPU/accelerator scheduling challenges, including gang-scheduling, topology-aware placement, preemption, and hardware affinity constraints.

Experience defining and enforcing SLAs and resource guarantees for compute workloads—including mechanisms to validate job prerequisites (data readiness, checkpoint availability, hardware compatibility) before scheduling to avoid wasted resources.
Capacity planning experience across cloud and on-premises infrastructure, including cost modeling, demand forecasting, and vendor management for compute procurement.
Scaled through hypergrowth in compute-intensive environments (AI/ML, HPC, large-scale cloud infrastructure).
Experience with observability and efficiency tooling for distributed infrastructure—building dashboards, automation, and governance workflows that drive utilization and cost accountability.

scope.surface: Compute Platform — scheduling, orchestration, and capacity management systems powering Anthropic's GPU and accelerator cluster infrastructure
scope.business_model: internal platform / infrastructure product serving research, engineering, and product teams
core_responsibilities: Partner with Infrastructure to build job scheduling, prioritization, and allocation systems across GPU and accelerator clusters
Define and own strategy and roadmap across job scheduling primitives, capacity allocation policies, preemption and fairness frameworks, quota management, and observability tooling
Understand needs of internal customers across Research, Infrastructure, Product, and Finance
Define and iterate on the semantic layer for job scheduling: abstractions, priority tiers, resource classes, and preemption policies
Partner with engineering leads to design scheduling capabilities that maximize cluster utilization while honoring resource guarantees
Drive product strategy for compute capacity management including quota systems, fairness policies, bin-packing optimizations, and gang-scheduling
Own the trade-off framework between utilization efficiency, job latency, cost, and reliability
Collaborate with Capacity Strategy & Operations on capacity planning models, demand forecasting, and cost-to-serve analytics
Build and champion observability tools and dashboards for real-time visibility into cluster health and scheduling efficiency

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($305K-$385K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with good-faith effort stated
Clear and specific product surface with well-articulated ownership scope
Encourages applications from underrepresented groups and candidates who don't meet every qualification
High-impact, foundational infrastructure role — directly affects all model training and inference at Anthropic
Cross-functional scope gives the PM significant leverage and visibility
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
7+ years PM experience combined with deep compute infrastructure domain knowledge is an unusually narrow intersection, making qualified candidates rare

Comp range upper bound ($385K) is notably high even for Anthropic, suggesting this may be a Staff/Principal-level role but JD does not explicitly state the level — seniority expectations may be even higher than the 7-year minimum implies
No explicit remote eligibility outside SF noted

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range $305K-$385K and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly titled Senior or Staff in JD)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated verbatim in JD)
location.cities_listed (SF inferred from 'headquartered in San Francisco' and hybrid policy; no other city explicitly named for this role)
equity_form (inferred from 'optional equity donation matching' in benefits section, implying equity is part of compensation)
ambiguities: The JD states 'Minimum years of experience: Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' — this is circular and non-informative; the 7+ years figure comes from the qualifications section and is treated as the operative minimum.
The role's seniority level is not named (Senior PM, Staff PM, Principal PM) in the JD. The compensation range ($305K-$385K) strongly implies Staff/Principal level, but this is inferred.
The JD logistics section says 'some roles may require more time in our offices' — it is unclear whether this compute infrastructure role falls into that category.
